did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper did not use AI, machine learning, or deep learning. It utilized optical coherence tomography (OCT) and OCT angiography (OCTA) imaging systems. While signal processing techniques such as dispersion compensation, shadow compensation, Gaussian spatial filtering, speckle variance calculation, Wiener filtering, and thresholding were applied to generate OCTA images, these are conventional computational image processing methods, not AI/ML models. The study relied on visual inspection and manual segmentation (marked with dashed lines) rather than automated AI-based diagnosis or classification.

non_compliance_reason: The paper does not use AI to obtain results in dermatology.
